my_thoughts: 'How do I do it?'—still trying to outsource but now it's method not decision. Progress: they own the WHAT (leave), now want me to own the HOW. Teacher blocks again: 'only you can figure out how.' Same move, different level. Can't have me decide AND execute. This maintains consistency: your life, your choices, your methods. They know what, they can figure out how. Hypothesis: they'll argue 'but I need help' or grudgingly accept.
accumulated_insight: T4: Student questions 'Then how do I do it?'—still seeking instruction but now about implementation not decision. From 'tell me what to do' (T1) to 'how do I do what I want' (T4). Teacher returns responsibility: 'Only you can figure out how.' Refuses procedural authority same as decision authority. Predicting resist_intellectually because they'll push for concrete steps.
visible_response:
I hear you, but, as long as you give up on yourself, this is the result you’ll have.
What can we do about this destructive habit of giving up on yourself?
